info.name: Azercell Marketplace
info.description: Catalog (admin + public) and Inventory/Warehousing endpoints. Requests auto-capture IDs into collection variables, so run them top-to-bottom: create -> get -> update.

item.name: Create Product
item.description: creditPlanIds: omit or pass [] -> product is eligible for ALL active credit plans (the default). Pass specific plan ids (see Financing -> List Credit Plans) to restrict; an unknown/inactive id returns 400 CREDIT_PLAN_NOT_ACTIVE. GET product detail returns installmentOptions (monthly + total per eligible plan) computed off the selling price (promo-aware).

item.name: Get Product By Id (admin)
item.description: Response includes installmentOptions: for each eligible credit plan, the monthlyInstallment + totalPayable for the product's selling price.

request.description: Storefront product detail. `installmentOptions` is the full per-plan breakdown computed for this product's selling price (months, monthlyInstallment, totalPayable, interestRate, name) — render the installment table directly, no extra calls needed. NOTE: the old raw `creditPlans` (Set<UUID>) field was removed; use `installmentOptions` instead.

item.name: Update Product
item.description: Run 'Get Product By Id (admin)' first so variantId/imageId are captured. Keeps the existing variant, edits its image, adds a new variant; the Blue variant from create gets removed.

query.description: Case-insensitive partial match on product name (e.g. iphone)

request.description:
Storefront product list (paginated). Each card now includes an installment hint: `monthlyFrom` (cheapest monthly installment across the product's eligible credit plans — the "from X/month" badge) and `maxMonths` (longest available term). Both are null when the product has no eligible plans. `colorOptions` drives the colour swatches (click a swatch -> show its `imageUrl`).

Optional filters (stackable): name (partial, case-insensitive), categoryId, minPrice, maxPrice. Price bounds apply to the effective selling price (COALESCE(promo, base)). Enable the disabled query params to use them.

Storefront product detail. `installmentOptions` is the full per-plan breakdown computed for this product's selling price (months, monthlyInstallment, totalPayable, interestRate, name) — render the installment table directly, no extra calls needed. NOTE: the old raw `creditPlans` (Set<UUID>) field was removed; use `installmentOptions` instead.

item.name: Catalog - Admin (Brands)
item.description: Admin brand management. `code` is normalized to UPPERCASE and must match ^[A-Z0-9]{2,20}$. Name and code are unique (case-insensitive). On update, `active` is optional (null leaves status unchanged).

request.description: Creates a brand. Returns 201 + the created brand. `code` auto-uppercased (appl -> APPL).
Updates name/code and optionally status (active=false -> IN_ACTIVE).

item.name: Catalog - Admin (Categories)
item.description: Admin category management. Hierarchical: optional `parentId` (null/omitted = root). `slug` is auto-lowercased and unique (case-insensitive). On update all fields are applied: omit/null `parentId` makes it a root; `active` is optional (null leaves status unchanged). Guards: parent must exist (CATEGORY_PARENT_NOT_FOUND), no self-parent (CATEGORY_SELF_PARENT), no cycles (CATEGORY_CIRCULAR_REFERENCE).

request.description: Creates a root category (no parentId). Returns 201 + the created category.
Full update. parentId null -> make root. active optional.

item.name: Inventory - Admin (Stock)
item.description: Stock is seeded automatically when a product is created (the variant's stockCount is seeded into the PRIMARY warehouse via an in-JVM event). Set a primary warehouse first (Warehouses -> Set Primary), then create a product, then run 'Get Product By Id (admin)' to capture variantId, then use the requests here. Restock/Adjust need warehouseId — set the {{warehouseId}} variable (e.g. via Create/Set-Primary warehouse) to the PRIMARY warehouse so it matches the seeded rows.

item.name: Financing - Credit Plans
item.description: Admin defines installment plans. The public 'Quote' endpoint computes monthly amounts for a price (storefront 'from X / month'). Interest is flat: interest = price x rate%, total = price + interest, monthly = total / months. type: DEFAULT = part of the standard offering, auto-applied to products that don't specify creditPlanIds; OPTIONAL = opt-in, only applied when a product explicitly lists it. The 5 standard plans (Full/3/6/9/12) are auto-seeded as DEFAULT on a fresh DB.

item.name: Create Credit Plan
item.description: type: omit -> OPTIONAL (safe default); set DEFAULT to add the plan to the standard offering. This sample creates an OPTIONAL plan, so it will NOT auto-apply to products unless explicitly assigned via creditPlanIds.

item.name: Get Order Credit (by order)
item.description: The financing agreement + installment schedule for an order (place an order WITH a creditPlanId first). Captures {{orderCreditId}} for the Pay request.

exec: if (b && b.data && b.data.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('orderCreditId', b.data.id); console.log('orderCreditId =', b.data.id); }

item.name: Pay Next Installment
item.description: Settles the earliest unpaid installment. Repeat until status = COMPLETED; paying a completed credit -> 400 ORDER_CREDIT_NOT_ACTIVE.

item.name: Orders
item.description: Place an order from variant lines (no cart yet). Prereqs: a PRIMARY warehouse + a product with stock + run 'Catalog -> Get Product By Id (admin)' to capture {{variantId}}. Placing reserves stock (RESERVE); admin transitions drive the lifecycle (SHIPPED consumes stock = SALE; CANCELLED while still held releases = RELEASE). userId/changedBy are placeholders until the users context exists.

exec: if (b && b.data && b.data.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('orderId', b.data.id); console.log('orderId =', b.data.id); }

request.description: Place an order for the authenticated employee. The user is taken from the Keycloak token (or the local dev-user in the 'local' profile) — no userId in the body. 'warehouseId' is optional; omit it to use the primary warehouse.

item.name: Place Order (with installments)
item.description: Same as Place Order but chooses a credit plan -> financing opens an OrderCredit + monthly schedule on the order total. Run a 'Financing -> Create Credit Plan' (sets {{creditPlanId}}) or any active plan id first. Then use 'Financing -> Get Order Credit (by order)'.

request.description: Same as Place Order but with an installment plan. Order total is financed via the chosen credit plan.
Lists orders for the authenticated user (resolved from the token / dev-user).

item.name: Update Order Status (admin)
item.description: Allowed: PENDING -> CONFIRMED/CANCELLED; CONFIRMED -> PROCESSING/CANCELLED; PROCESSING -> SHIPPED/CANCELLED; SHIPPED -> DELIVERED. SHIPPED consumes reserved stock; CANCELLED (while held) releases it. Illegal transitions -> 400.

item.name: Users (Identity)
item.description: Backend is an OAuth2 resource server over RHSSO Keycloak. It does NOT handle login/registration/passwords — the frontend authenticates against Keycloak and sends the access token here as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'. On the first authenticated request the local user mirror is JIT-provisioned, keyed by the OIDC 'sub' claim (users.ad_object_id). In the 'local' profile there is no token; a seeded dev user is used (app.security.dev-user-fallback=true).

request.description: Returns the current authenticated user (JIT-provisioned local mirror). In qa/prod enable the Authorization header and set {{accessToken}} to a Keycloak token; in local it works without a token.
